Japanese: ６月２２日　午前　９時５１分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: June 22, 9:51 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: ‥‥あのさ。なるほどくん。
English: Um... Nick...

Japanese: ん？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: あの子‥‥２代目狩魔検事、 １８才なんだって。知ってた？
English: Did you know that Von Karma's daughter is only 18... Just like me...?

Japanese: あ、そうなんだ。 ‥‥それがどうかした？
English: Um, yeah. ...Why?

Japanese: すごいな、って思って。
English: I was just thinking about how strong she is...

Japanese: 今までアメリカで、たったひとりで がんばってきたんだよ。
English: I mean, she's been in Germany all this time all by herself, and she's so grown up!

Japanese: ‥‥うーん。きっと、すごい プレッシャーがあっただろうね。
English: ...Yeah, and I'm sure she felt a lot of pressure from her father's reputation.

Japanese: ‥‥それにひきかえ、 あたしときたら。
English: ...And then you look at me, and, well...

Japanese: 家元のムスメなのに、 なんか中途ハンパ。
English: I'm the daughter of the Master, but I'm still just a little girl.

Japanese: おまけに、何かといえばハンニン あつかいでタイホされちゃうし。
English: And on top of that, I'm the suspect in a murder trial.

Japanese: （うーん‥‥じゅうぶんスゴいと 思うけどな、真宵ちゃんも‥‥）
English: (Um... But I think you're really strong too, Maya, for all you've gone through...)

Japanese: おはようございます！
English: Good morning!

Japanese: 今日もお２人、なかよしですね。 ‥‥わたくし、うれしいです。
English: The two of you look like you're doing well today. I'm happy for you!

Japanese: あ、はみちゃん！ おはよう。
English: Hey! Morning, Pearly!

Japanese: 真宵さま、 もう安心ですからね！
English: You'll be safe today, Mystic Maya!
Huh? What do you mean?

Japanese: 今日は、おかあさまが 味方になってくれますから！
English: My mother is coming today to show her support!

Japanese: う‥‥うん。そうだよね！
English: Y-Yeah... Yeah! That's right, Pearly!

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。
English: Hey, Pearls...

Japanese: ‥‥？ なんですか、なるほどくん。
English: Yes, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: あのさ。今日も 呼んでくれないかな？　千尋さん。
English: Um, do you think you could do me a big favor? Could you channel Mia today too?

Japanese: え？　‥‥できればわたくし、 お席のほうで裁判の見学を‥‥
English: Huh? ...I was sort of hoping to sit in the audience today and watch...

Japanese: ‥‥たのむよ。 自信がないんだ、ぼくだけじゃ。
English: ...Please. It's very important to me. I don't feel confident enough without her help...

Japanese: もう。ダメですよ、なるほどくん。
English: Mr. Nick! That's enough!

Japanese: 愛しいヒトの前で 殿方が弱音を吐いてしまっては。
English: You can't show weakness in front of the person you love!

Japanese: は、はみちゃん！ ま、またそんなコト‥‥。
English: P-Pearly! N-Not this again...

Japanese: おねがいできるかな？ 春美ちゃん。
English: Please, can you do this for us, Pearls?

Japanese: ‥‥ふふ。いいですよ。 真宵さまのためですから。
English: Um... Alright. I'll do it. I'll do it for Mystic Maya's sake.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあわたくし、 したくしてきますね！
English: See you later, then! I'll leave you two to your alone time!

Japanese: ‥‥これで、よし。
English: ...Phew, thank goodness.

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥？
English: Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥これで春美ちゃんは、 今日の裁判を聞くことはできない。
English: This way, Pearls won't be able to see today's trial...

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どういうこと‥‥？
English: ...Wh-What do you mean...? What's going on...?

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: June 22, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: これより、綾里　真宵の法廷を 開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Maya Fey.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトは どうでもよろしい。
English: Let's start already.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一。 今夜のニュースが楽しみね。
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright. I look forward to tonight's news with great joy.

Japanese: 全世界に流れるわ。 ‥‥あなたの敗北するすがたが！
English: The image of your defeated face will be transmitted all over the world!

Japanese: 全世界‥‥。有名になった ものね、なるほどくん。
English: All over the world, huh? Sounds like you've made quite a name for yourself, Phoenix.

Japanese: ‥‥バカな服を着たバカに ふさわしいバカなカンちがいね。
English: Don't be foolish, you foolish fool wearing the foolishly foolish clothes.

Japanese: 有名なのは、この私。
English: The famous one is me!

Japanese: １３才から５年間、 無敗の天才なのよ。
English: I'm the prodigy who has never lost a case since becoming a prosecutor five years ago.

Japanese: その私が、この国で初勝利する。 全世界が注目しているわ！
English: Naturally, the world's eyes are on me, as I conduct my first trial in this country!

Japanese: それはそれは‥‥。 おキノドクにね。
English: Uh, huh... That's nice, Ms. von Karma.

Japanese: ‥‥フン。イセイのいい お姉さんだこと。
English: Hmph. Glad to see you're in such good "spirits" today, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: （うう‥‥オンナの戦いは コワいな‥‥）
English: (Uhh... It's true what they say... Women really are scary when they fight... *gulp*)

Japanese: ‥‥さて。きのうの法廷で、 ある可能性が示されました。
English: Now then. A very interesting theory was presented during yesterday's session.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人が、“対面の間”から 出たかもしれないという可能性。
English: That the defendant could have left the Channeling Chamber, correct?

Japanese: まさしく。このカギが、 その証拠です。
English: Yes. And this key is proof of that.

Japanese: “対面の間”にあるはずのカギが、 他の場所から見つかりました。
English: This key, the only key to the Channeling Chamber, was not where it should have been.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。最初に１つ、 言っておくことがあるの。
English: Your Honor, I would like to say one thing before we begin.

Japanese: ほ、ほう‥‥。 なんでしょうか。
English: A-Alright... Let's hear it.

Japanese: 事件発生から逮捕までのあいだ、 被告人は一度、あの部屋を出た‥‥
English: The prosecution has determined that from the time of the murder to the time of arrest,

Japanese: 検察側は、それを ジジツとして認めるわ。
English: the defendant did indeed leave the room at one point.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: しかし‥‥狩魔検事！
English: But... Ms. von Karma!

Japanese: では、この写真は どう説明するつもりですか？
English: Then... How do you explain this picture?

Japanese: この人物も、 被告ではなかった、と‥‥？
English: Are you saying then, that the person in this picture is not the defendant...?

Japanese: ‥‥私は被告が 無実だとは言っていない。
English: I never said that wasn't the defendant.

Japanese: で、ではいったいどういう‥‥？
English: Th-Then what is the meaning of this...?

Japanese: 綾里　真宵は被害者を殺害後、 あの部屋から出たのよ。
English: All I am saying is that Maya Fey, after killing the victim, exited the room.

Japanese: カギは、そのとき落とした と考えられるわ。
English: And I believe that is when she dropped this key.

Japanese: ‥‥それを 立証できるのですか？
English: Can you substantiate your claim?

Japanese: そのために私はここにいる。
English: Isn't that what I'm here for?

Japanese: 証人として、被告人のおば、 綾里　キミ子を入廷させるわ。
English: The prosecution would like to call the defendant's aunt, Morgan Fey, to the stand.

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、おばさまが‥‥
English: ...Just as I suspected. Aunt Morgan...

Japanese: （千尋さんもツラそうだ‥‥）
English: (Poor Mia... She seems really torn by this...)

Japanese: 証人。‥‥名前と職業を。
English: Witness. Name and occupation.

Japanese: 綾里　キミ子と申します。 職業は、霊媒を少々‥‥
English: My name is Morgan Fey, and I am a spirit medium, in a manner of speaking...

Japanese: し、シツレイですが、 ナニを少々ですって‥‥？
English: I'm sorry, but what do you mean, "in a manner of speaking"...?

Japanese: きゃん！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトは どうでもよろしい。
English: I don't think anyone really cares.

Japanese: 事件があったとき、証人は 被告の介抱をしたわね？
English: Now then, after the murder took place, you kept watch over the defendant, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥そうでござあますわね。
English: Yes, that is correct.

Japanese: 真宵さまに除霊の術を ほどこしたのでござあます。
English: I performed the Spirit Severing Technique on Mystic Maya then.

Japanese: じょ、じょれいのじゅつ‥‥？
English: S-Spirit Severing Technique?

Japanese: 体内に宿った霊を、冥界に おくる術でござあますが、何か？
English: A technique to remove a spirit from a body and send it back to the other world. That is...

Japanese: な、“ナニか？”ってあなた‥‥
English: Yes...? That is...?

Japanese: むふっ！
English: Mmfph!

Japanese: あなたはダマってなさいな。
English: You. Be quiet.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、証人。
English: Now, witness.

Japanese: 除霊の術をほどこしたときに、 何かあったんでしょ？
English: Something happened while you were performing this technique, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: ...Yes, that is correct.

Japanese: い、いったい、何が‥‥？
English: Wh-What in the world could've...!?

Japanese: お逃げになったのです。 ‥‥真宵さまが。
English: Mystic Maya... She escaped from the room...

Japanese: なんですって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: And here we come to the heart of the matter.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵は、霊が宿ったまま “対面の間”から消えた！
English: Maya Fey, while in a possessed state, managed to escape from the Channeling Chamber!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: 証人！　そのときのことを 証言してください！
English: Ms. Fey! Please testify to this court what happened during that time!

Japanese: ‥‥おおせのままに いたしましょう。
English: Your Honor, I will try my best...

Japanese: いきなり 盛り上がってきたわね‥‥。
English: I think someone just upped the ante on this trial...

Japanese: ～真宵を逃がしたこと～
English: -- Maya's Escape --

Japanese: 銃声が聞こえて、おふたりが 扉をこわして現場に入られました。
English: After we heard the gunshots, those two broke the door open and entered the Chamber.

Japanese: アタクシ、成歩堂さんたちに警察を 呼ぶようおねがいしました。
English: I requested that Mr. Wright and the other lady please contact the police.

Japanese: 真宵さまはピストルをぶら下げて 放心しておられたのですが‥‥
English: A pistol was hanging from Mystic Maya's hand and she was in a daze...

Japanese: いきなりアタクシをつきとばして、 部屋を出てしまわれたのです。
English: Then, quite suddenly, she thrusted me away from herself and escaped from the room.

Japanese: すごいイキオイで首スジを たたかれて、少し気絶して‥‥
English: With great strength, she hit the base of my neck and I fainted for a short while...

Japanese: 真宵さまがどこへ行かれたかは、 存じません。
English: I'm afraid I have no knowledge of where she went after that.

Japanese: どうして、今まで かくしていたんですか！
English: Why have you hidden this until now!?

Japanese: 真宵さまに‥‥ごメイワクを かけたくなかったのでござあます。
English: I... I did not wish to cause more trouble for Mystic Maya.

Japanese: しかし‥‥アヤマチは ただされなくてはなりません。
English: However... I must correct falsities when they arise.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。 ただされることね、成歩堂　龍一。
English: That's right. She is simply correcting a falsity, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: （なんでぼくなんだよ‥‥）
English: (Why is she making it a point to take a stab at me... Never mind.)

Japanese: では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright, your cross- examination, please.

Japanese: ‥‥おばさまは、とても アタマのいい人よ。
English: My aunt is a very smart and sly person.

Japanese: つけいるスキを 作ってくれるかしらね‥‥
English: It's going to be hard for us to find a crack in her testimony...

Japanese: ～真宵を逃がしたこと～
English: -- Maya's Escape --

Japanese: 銃声が聞こえて、おふたりが 扉をこわして現場に入られました。
English: After we heard the gunshots, those two broke the door open and entered the Chamber.

Japanese: アタクシ、成歩堂さんたちに警察を 呼ぶようおねがいしました。
English: I requested that Mr. Wright and the other lady please contact the police.

Japanese: 真宵さまはピストルをぶら下げて 放心しておられたのですが‥‥
English: A pistol was hanging from Mystic Maya's hand and she was in a daze...

Japanese: いきなりアタクシをつきとばして、 部屋を出てしまわれたのです。
English: Then, quite suddenly, she thrusted me away from herself and escaped from the room.

Japanese: すごいイキオイで首スジを たたかれて、少し気絶して‥‥
English: With great strength, she hit the base of my neck and I fainted for a short while...

Japanese: 真宵さまがどこへ行かれたかは、 存じません。
English: I'm afraid I have no knowledge of where she went after that.

Japanese: この証言‥‥ スキが見あたらないわ。
English: I can't see a crack in her testimony...

Japanese: ど、どうしましょう‥‥。
English: Wh-What should we do...?

Japanese: おそらくこれは、次の証言に そなえての“準備”みたいなもの。
English: For now, we should try and get more information out of her.

Japanese: 今はとにかく、情報を 集めておくしかないようね。
English: After we get more information from her we may be able to use it later to our advantage.

Japanese: “おふたり”というのは‥‥
English: Just now, you said, "those two." Who were the two people you were referring to?

Japanese: ああたと、あの外国人の 女のかたでござあますわね。
English: I was referring to that foreign lady and yourself, good sir.

Japanese: ‥‥ナツミさんは ニッポン人ですけど‥‥。
English: Um, Lotta's not a foreigner... despite how she talks...

Japanese: そうなのでござあますか。
English: Oh, is that so?

Japanese: 見苦しい身なりに 聞き苦しい日本語でしたので、
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: アタクシはてっきり‥‥
English: I simply could not understand her atrocious English, not to mention she looked awful!

Japanese: （‥‥関西の黒人ふうの ヒトたちに怒られるぞ‥‥）
English: (I can already see people in the heartland gearing up for a riot...)

Japanese: とにかく、ドアの修理代は 事務所に請求いたしましたので。
English: In any case, I have already sent the repair bill to your office, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: そ、それはどうも。
English: Oh, uh, thanks.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥それから、 証人はどうしましたか？
English: Hmm... What did you do after that, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: 電話をかけるだけなのに、 なぜ２人を行かせたのですか？
English: Was it really necessary to have two people do something so simple as call the police?

Japanese: 逃げられる可能性があったのなら、 どちらかを残すべきでした！
English: If there was a possibility of escape, then one of us should have stayed behind!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ たしかに、そのとおりですな。
English: Hmm, yes. That is a very good point.

Japanese: あのときはアタクシも 動転しておりました。
English: At that time, I, myself, was a little confused.

Japanese: それに、あの。 なんと言いますか‥‥。
English: On top of which, there was another...

Japanese: なんですか？
English: Another what, madam?

Japanese: ‥‥いえ。 それだけでござあます。
English: Oh, it's nothing. No need to concern yourself.

Japanese: （‥‥少しワザとらしく 口ごもったみたいだけど‥‥）
English: (I want to hear the end of that sentence...)

Japanese: 何か、他にも 理由があったんですね！
English: There was another reason you wanted both of us out of that room, wasn't there!?

Japanese: 証言してください。なぜ、 ぼくたちを追い払ったんですか！
English: You must testify! Why did you chase us out of the room!?

Japanese: ‥‥あの医者の他に、ギセイ者を 出したくありませんでしたの。
English: ...I simply did not wish for there to be more victims.

Japanese: “ああたや、あのカメラマンの 命も危ないかもしれない”‥‥
English: "The lives of both the good sir and that camera woman may be in danger..."

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えたのでござあます。
English: is what I thought to myself.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥さすが、 おエラいココロがけですな。
English: Well, that was very noble of you, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: （まずいな‥‥悪い印象を 与えちまった）
English: (This is bad... Now people have a good impression of her and a bad one of us...)

Japanese: ワナだったみたいね。 ‥‥さすが、おばさまだわ。
English: Looks like she caught us napping. That's my aunt for you...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、じゅうぶんでしょう。 証人。つづきをおねがいします。
English: Are you satisfied now, Mr. Wright? Ms. Fey, please continue.

Japanese: （口ごもりかたが、 どうもウソっぽかった‥‥）
English: (The last bit of her sentence sounds like a red herring...)

Japanese: （ワナかもしれない。 深追いするのはやめておこう）
English: (If that's the case, I'm not going to fall into her trap today.)

Japanese: では。質問もないようなので、 先に進みましょう。
English: If the defense has no further questions, let us press on.

Japanese: あなたは、あのとき たしか、こう言ってました。
English: During our investigation, you stated that you struck the defendant on the head,

Japanese: 『すぐに被告人に当て身をして、 気絶させた』‥‥と。
English: and this strike caused Maya to lose conciousness. Do you stand by this statement?

Japanese: あれは‥‥ ウソでござあました。
English: I'm afraid I don't. The statement I gave you was a lie...

Japanese: ど、どうしてそんなウソを！
English: B-But why would you lie about something like that!?

Japanese: ‥‥アタクシの口からは申し上げ にくいのでござあますが‥‥。
English: It is very painful for me to say this, however...

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、被告人を かばうため、ウソをついたのよ。
English: The witness was lying to cover for the defendant.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんを‥‥かばうため？）
English: (She was... covering for Maya...?)

Japanese: “被告をかばう”‥‥ それは、どういう意味ですか！
English: So, you were "covering for the defendant"!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうでござあますわねえ。 あとで知ったのでござあますが、
English: Well, yes... It was only later that I was informed of what had occurred.

Japanese: 逃げた真宵さまは、 とんでもないことをなさって‥‥
English: That the renegade Mystic Maya had done something very unexpected...

Japanese: と、“とんでもないこと”！ それはいったい‥‥
English: S-Something unexpected!? What, pray tell, did sh--

Japanese: ふひっ！
English: Oogh!

Japanese: この証人は、それを 目撃しているわけではない。
English: This witness didn't and couldn't have seen what happened.

Japanese: “とんでもないこと”については、 のちほど説明するわ。
English: If you want to know what this unexpected thing was, there is always later.

Japanese: は‥‥はい。わかりました。
English: Y-Yes, very well...

Japanese: （うう‥‥ イヤな予感がするなあ‥‥）
English: (Ugh... Why does it suddenly feel like there's a rock in the pit of my stomach...?)

Japanese: じゃあ、証人。何があったか、 証言をつづけて。
English: Now, witness, continue with what happened.

Japanese: （検察側に有利なことなら、 狩魔が証言させるはずだ）
English: (I'm sure Von Karma's allowing this testimony because it's advantageous to her...)

Japanese: （‥‥ここは、 ゆさぶる必要はないな‥‥）
English: (...Which means I shouldn't press Morgan any further...)

Japanese: ‥‥今にわかるわ、成歩堂　龍一。 今に、ね‥‥。
English: You're finally beginning to understand, aren't you, Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: じゃあ、証人。何があったか、 証言をつづけなさい。
English: Now, witness, continue with what happened.

Japanese: アッサリ、逃げられて しまったんですか？
English: Was it really that easy for her to escape from you?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。メンボクない 話でござあますわね。
English: I'm ashamed to admit to such a thing, however, yes...

Japanese: あなたは、被告よりも 体格がいい。
English: But you are physically larger and stronger than the defendant.

Japanese: そうカンタンに 逃げられるとは‥‥
English: There is no way she could have escaped from you so easily...

Japanese: お忘れみたいね‥‥成歩堂　龍一。 この写真をごらんなさい。
English: Have you forgotten already, Mr. Phoenix Wright? What about this picture!?

Japanese: そのとき被告人は、“綾里　真宵” ではなかったのよ！
English: Remember that at the time, the defendant was not physically "Maya Fey".

Japanese: ‥‥うぐっ！
English: ...Ack!

Japanese: うむむ‥‥。年寄りには ややこしい話ですなあ。
English: Hmm... Growing old is such a mysterious thing. Trust me, I know first-hand.

Japanese: おそらく、あの看護師は生前、 カラテでもやっていたのでしょう。
English: Basically, Mystic Maya's body was still that of the nurse she had called.

Japanese: ‥‥でも！　逃げられたのなら、 すぐに追うべきだった！
English: But if she had run away, wouldn't you have given chase right away!?

Japanese: ええ。たしかに そのとおりでござあましたが‥‥
English: Well, yes, I would have... however...

Japanese: ‥‥キゼツ、ですか？
English: You fainted...?

Japanese: 目の前がクラクラっとして ‥‥倒れてしまいましたの。
English: I became dizzy, and then collapsed onto the floor.

Japanese: 気絶されていた時間は どのぐらいでしたか？
English: How long were you unconscious?

Japanese: さあ‥‥。たぶん、１０分ぐらいの ことでござあましょう。
English: I... I'm not sure. About ten minutes, perhaps.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 被告人はそのあいだ、どこへ行って いたのでしょうねえ‥‥。
English: And the defendant almost certainly went somewhere in that time...

Japanese: いかが？　証人。 ‥‥その点については。
English: Happy? Witness, elaborate on that point for us.

Japanese: 気絶していたから、ですか？
English: Is that because you were unconscious?

Japanese: ‥‥さようでござあます。
English: Why, yes...

Japanese: （‥‥どうしよう‥‥？）
English: (Great, well, now what...?)

Japanese: 気絶していたのなら、被告の行動は まったくわからないんですね？
English: So while you were unconscious, you have absolutely no idea where the defendant went?

Japanese: もちろんでござあます。
English: I would think that's obvious, good sir.

Japanese: それなら、被告が“対面の間”から 出ていったかどうか‥‥、
English: If that's true...

Japanese: あなたにはわからないはずです！
English: then how can you know for sure that the defendant left the Channeling Chamber at all!?

Japanese: 言われてみれば ‥‥そのとおりです！
English: Now that you mention it... I have to wonder myself!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、この証人には 知りようがない。
English: It's true that this witness is not able to confirm this.

Japanese: “この証人には”‥‥ね。
English: But the key word here is "this" witness.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか！
English: What do you mean by that!?

Japanese: ‥‥他の証人がいる。 そういうことでしょうね。
English: It means she has another witness up her sleeve. That's what.

Japanese: どうやら、あの検事さんは 次の証人を用意してるみたい。
English: It looks like Ms. von Karma is ready to move on to her next witness.

Japanese: （気絶していたのなら、 これ以上の証言は不可能だ）
English: (If she fainted, then there's no way she can testify any more than she has...)

Japanese: （ゆさぶってみても、 意味はないな‥‥）
English: (If I press her, I'm not going to get anything useful anyway...)

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥。では、
English: Hmm... Well, then...

Japanese: 証言をつづけてもらいましょう。
English: Let's continue on with the testimony, shall we?

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、証言には 問題ないようです。
English: From what I can tell, there is nothing wrong with this witness' testimony.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵を現場から逃がして しまった事情は、わかりました。
English: And from what she has said, we can establish that Maya Fey did leave the Chamber.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥ これでよかったのかな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I hope this isn't going to come back and bite us in the butt...)

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、 次のステップに進むわ。
English: Now, let's take the next logical step and ask,

Japanese: “現場から消えた被告が、 どこへ向かったか‥‥？”
English: "Where did the defendant go after leaving the Channeling Chamber?"

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですね。 やはり、気になるのは‥‥
English: Yes, yes... That is exactly what we should b--

Japanese: かはっ！
English: Eeek!

Japanese: ‥‥口を挟まない。
English: Be quiet, you!

Japanese: 逃げだした被告は、 ある人物と会話をしていたのよ！
English: Now then, what the escapee had done was she had gone to speak with a certain person!

Japanese: か‥‥会話だって！
English: She... She went to speak with someone!?

Japanese: そ‥‥その人物とは、 いったいだれですか！
English: Wh-Who was it!?

Japanese: 事件当日、《控えの間》にいた 葉中　のどかを入廷させなさい。
English: The prosecution calls Ms. Ini Miney, who was sleeping in the Side Room at the time.

Japanese: の、のどかさん‥‥？
English: I-Ini Miney...?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら 話が見えてきたわね‥‥。
English: I think you can see where this is headed...

Japanese: 証人。名前と職業を。
English: Witness. Name and occupation.

Japanese: ええとお、お名前はあ‥‥ 葉中　のどかでえす。
English: Um... OK, so my name is, like, Ini Miney.

Japanese: あのお、大学でえ、超心理学を 研究していまあす。
English: I'm, like, researching, like, parapsychology stuff at the, um, university.

Japanese: ちょうしんりがく？
English: What is this "parapsychology"?

Japanese: ええとお‥‥オカルトですねえ、 ひらたく言えば。
English: Um... Let's see... It's, like, I guess, most people call it "occult stuff".

Japanese: ひらたく言われても リカイできません。
English: Even if that's what most people call it, I can't say I understand what that means...

Japanese: ウチに帰って 自分で調べなさい。
English: Then I suggest you go home and research it yourself.

Japanese: は。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥さて、証人。
English: Now then, Ms. Miney,

Japanese: あなたは事件直後、 被告人・綾里　真宵と対面した。
English: after the murder took place, you spoke with the defendant, Maya Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいないかしら？
English: Is this correct?

Japanese: そうですねえ‥‥。 ええっとお‥‥
English: Um... Well... Hmm... Like, I guess...

Japanese: まちがいない、と。 ‥‥じゃ、証言してもらうわ。
English: If that's the case, then let's hear your testimony.

Japanese: いいわね、裁判長。
English: That IS alright, isn't it, Your Honor?

Japanese: は。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: （いいのか、裁判長！）
English: (For the love of all things good, Your Honor, have some spine... For my sake...)

Japanese: ～事件の直後、目撃したこと～
English: -- After the Murder --

Japanese: 霊媒が始まったとき、私、 “控えの間”で寝ていましたあ。
English: Like, when the channeling started, I was, like, sleeping in the Side Room.

Japanese: しばらくしたら、急にだれかが 部屋に入ってきたんですう。
English: Like, a little later, someone came into the room, like, really suddenly.

Japanese: ‥‥それは‥‥ お、お姉さんだったんです！
English: It was, like, oh my gawd, totally my sister!

Japanese: 私、なつかしくってえ‥‥、 うれしかったんですう。でも。
English: I, like, hadn't seen her in, like, so long... I was so happy in, like, a sad way...

Japanese: お姉さん‥‥私に、 おそろしいことを話したんです。
English: My sister... She, like, told me something, like, totally terrible.

Japanese: ちょちょ、ちょっと待ちなさい！ 部屋に“お姉さんが入ってきた”？
English: Now hold your horses, young lady! You're saying the person that entered was your sister!?

Japanese: 被告人‥‥“綾里　真宵”の まちがいではないのですか？
English: Don't you mean the defendant, Maya Fey...?

Japanese: いいかげん、認めるの ‥‥裁判長。
English: Really, now... Your Honor.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵はそのとき、 霊媒中だったのよ。
English: Maya Fey was still in the middle of channeling at that time.

Japanese: その霊が‥‥この証人の お姉さんだった、というのですか！
English: A-Are you saying that the spirit was the spirit of this witness' sister!?

Japanese: ‥‥葉中　未実。霧崎医師の 病院で、看護師をしていたわ。
English: Yes, Mimi Miney. She was a nurse at Dr. Grey's clinic.

Japanese: な‥‥なんとまあ‥‥
English: Oh... W-Well... This is...

Japanese: 証人。
English: Witness.

Japanese: はあい。
English: Like, yeah?

Japanese: あなたは“おそろしいこと”を 聞いたと証言した。
English: In your testimony, you mentioned a "terrible" thing.

Japanese: いったいそれは、 どんなことだったのか‥‥？
English: Why don't you tell the court what this terrible thing was?

Japanese: 私たちに教えて。
English: I'm sure we'd love to hear about it.

Japanese: ‥‥あのお‥‥どうしても、 言わなくちゃダメですかあ？
English: ...Um, like... Do I totally, like, really have to?

Japanese: ダメ。
English: Of course.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 おねがいします。
English: Ms. Miney... please.

Japanese: お姉さん‥‥私に、 こんなことを言ったんですう。
English: My sister... Like, this is what she said to me.

Japanese: ‥‥睡眠薬を飲まされたの‥‥
English: That was... no accident...

Japanese: あれは‥‥事故じゃなかったわ
English: I was drugged... with sleeping pills...

Japanese: あたしは‥‥殺されたのよ ‥‥アイツに‥‥
English: I was murdered... by that person...

Japanese: ‥‥だから‥‥復讐してやった‥‥
English: That's why... I took... my revenge...

Japanese: 当然でしょ？　のどか‥‥
English: It's only fair... Isn't it... ...Ini?

Japanese: “復讐してやった”‥‥
English: She "took her revenge?"

Japanese: まちがいなく、 そう言ったんですか？
English: Are you sure that's what she said?

Japanese: はあい‥‥。
English: Y-Yes...

Japanese: いかが？　裁判長。
English: There, are you satisfied, Your Honor?

Japanese: ‥‥私には、信じられません。
English: ...I still can't believe it.

Japanese: 霊媒師にとりついた霊が、自分を 殺した人物に復讐するなんて‥‥
English: I can't believe that a spirit would go so far as to use a medium to get revenge...

Japanese: たしかに、信じがたいわ。
English: It is a bit hard to swallow,

Japanese: しかし、すべての証拠や証言は、 それが事実だったと叫んでいる！
English: however, all the evidence and testimonies point to this as the truth!

Japanese: ‥‥以上。
English: ...The end.

Japanese: ‥‥どう思いますか？　弁護人。
English: ...What do you think, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: たしかに、尋問の余地は ないと思いますが‥‥。
English: It certainly seems like all the loose ends are accounted for...

Japanese: （これが‥‥）
English: (This...)

Japanese: （今の証言がすべて、 作り話だっていうのか‥‥？）
English: (This testimony just now... Was it all made up? Was it just one huge lie...?)

Japanese: 当然でしょ、なるほどくん。
English: Of course it was, Phoenix.

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さん！
English: ...Mia! What do you mean?

Japanese: よくできた作り話。‥‥でも、 それ以上のものではない。
English: It's well constructed... but that's all it is.

Japanese: たたきつぶしてやりましょう。 ‥‥こんな証言。
English: But even the most well-spun lies can be undone.

Japanese: できるはずでしょ。 ‥‥私たちなら！
English: We can do it, Phoenix. Let's find that one loose thread and unwind this tapestry of lies.

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！　弁護側の 尋問を聞いてください。
English: Your Honor! There is room for doubt, so the defense will cross-examine this witness.

Japanese: それで、すべてを 明らかにして見せます！
English: We believe that our cross- examination will reveal the real truth behind this murder!

Japanese: バカのバカによるバカのための バカさわぎ‥‥
English: Yet again the foolish fool spouts out more foolishly foolish drabble...

Japanese: ‥‥せいぜい、楽しませて もらおうかしら‥‥。
English: I wonder if you'll make this one more entertaining than the last...

Japanese: ～事件の直後、目撃したこと～
English: -- After the Murder --

Japanese: 霊媒が始まったとき、私、 “控えの間”で寝ていましたあ。
English: Like, when the channeling started, I was, like, sleeping in the Side Room.

Japanese: しばらくしたら、急にだれかが 部屋に入ってきたんですう。
English: Like, a little later, someone came into the room, like, really suddenly.

Japanese: ‥‥それは‥‥ お、お姉さんだったんです！
English: It was, like, oh my gawd, totally my sister!

Japanese: お姉さん‥‥私に、 おそろしいことを話したんです。
English: My sister... She, like, told me something, like, totally terrible.

Japanese: （‥‥いったい‥‥どこが ムジュンしているんだ？）
English: (I know there has to be something here I can use... But where!?)

Japanese: ‥‥そうとう考えられた ウソのようね。
English: She really put a lot of thought into this lie, didn't she?

Japanese: あせらないで。 じっくり行きましょう。
English: We can't rush this. Let's take it slowly, one step at a time.

Japanese: あの子はかならず、 どこかでミスをするわ。
English: She's bound to slip up somewhere.

Japanese: あきらめないで。 ‥‥そして、油断しないでね。
English: Don't give up. But don't get careless, OK? Remember, calm and collected.

Japanese: こわくはなかったです。装束にも あやしいところはなかったし。
English: I wasn't, like, scared at all. And, like, her costume looked totally, like, normal.

Japanese: 私、なつかしくってえ‥‥、 うれしかったんですう。でも。
English: I, like, hadn't seen her in, like, so long... I was so happy in, like, a sad way...

Japanese: たしか、ソバの アレルギーだった、とか‥‥。
English: Was that because of your, um... sesame allergy, was it?

Japanese: そうなんですよお。
English: Like, that's right.

Japanese: おヒルにいただいたダンゴの中に ひそんでたんです、ソバ粉が。
English: I, like, think there were sesame seeds, like, in the lunch they served that day.

Japanese: これぞまさしく“一寸先はソバ” って感じですよねえ。
English: I had this, like, premonition that it was going to totally be a "seedy" day.

Japanese: ‥‥まさしく、ね。
English: Yes, indeed.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほど‥‥。
English: Hmm... I see...

Japanese: （‥‥もしかして、ぼくだけか？ イミがわからないの‥‥）
English: (...Am I the only one who missed the boat here...?)

Japanese: とにかく、あなたは寝ていた、と。 ‥‥そして、何があったんですか？
English: Anyway, so you were taking a nap. Did something happen while you were sleeping?

Japanese: 時間はわかりませんか？
English: Do you know the time?

Japanese: ‥‥えっとお‥‥ １１時ちょっと前、ぐらいですね。
English: Um, uh... Like, maybe a little before 11 AM? Something like that, I think...
E-Eleven AM...?

Japanese: ええ。‥‥そろそろ、おなか へってきましたねえ。
English: Yeah. Like, my stomach is totally ready for, like, food.

Japanese: いやいやいや。今、何時かを 聞いたんじゃありませんよ。
English: Ah, no... I think you misunderstood me... I'm not asking what time it is now...

Japanese: その“だれか”が入ってきた 時間です！
English: I meant what time was it when the "someone" came into your room!

Japanese: ああ、そういうことですかあ。 ちゃんと言っていただかないとお。
English: Oh! So, like, that's what you meant! Like, you shoulda been more, like, clear about it!

Japanese: はあ、すみません。
English: S-Sorry. My bad.

Japanese: いいえぇ。 いいんですけどお‥‥。
English: No, it's OK. Like, it's really...

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんでそこで 会話が終わっちゃうんですか！
English: ...That's it? Why'd you stop mid-sentence!?

Japanese: あ。そうでした。 ‥‥で、なんでしたっけえ？
English: Did I? ...So, like... what was I talking about again...?

Japanese: も、もういいです。
English: N-Never mind...

Japanese: ‥‥証人。“控えの間”に 入ってきたのは‥‥？
English: ...Witness. About this person who entered the Side Room...

Japanese: まちがいありませんか？
English: Are you absolutely sure?

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: ...I guess?

Japanese: “はい？”じゃありませんよ。
English: You "guess"...?

Japanese: 本当に、あなたの お姉さんだったんですか？
English: Was it or was it not your sister who entered?

Japanese: そりゃそうですよお。
English: Like, I don't know.

Japanese: だって私、田中さんの お姉さん、知りませんしい。
English: I mean, like, I totally don't know "your sister" at all, Mr. Smith.

Japanese: （まず、ぼくは田中さんじゃない。 そして、ぼくはひとりっ子だ）
English: (First off, my name is not Mr. Smith. Second, I'm an only child.)

Japanese: （‥‥それ以前に、ビミョーに 質問の答えがズレてるし‥‥）
English: (...Hmm, but more than that, she's managed to mess up the question with her answer...)

Japanese: どうしました？　弁護人。
English: Well, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: つ‥‥つっこみきれません‥‥。
English: Th-The defense rests...

Japanese: 死ぬまでなやんでなさい。
English: Well, that was a colossal waste of our time!

Japanese: ‥‥それで、証人。葉中　未実に 会って、どう感じたの？
English: Now then, witness. How did you feel when you saw Ms. Mimi Miney?

Japanese: ‥‥なつかしかった？
English: You were... happy in a sad way...?

Japanese: ええ。だってえ、 お姉さんですからねえ。
English: Yeah, like, I mean, it was my sister!

Japanese: ふつう、おどろきませんか？ 亡くなったお姉さんが現れたら。
English: But wouldn't most people react here with surprise at seeing a dead loved one alive again?

Japanese: うーん。シロートさんなら そうかもしれませんねえ。
English: Um, but, like, maybe if I was, like, someone who didn't know about, like, the occult.

Japanese: でも私、倉院流霊媒道のコト、 知ってましたからあ。
English: But I, like, know all about the Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: とにかく証人は、すぐにそれが “姉”だとわかったわけね。
English: The point here, is the witness immediately recognized that it was her "sister".

Japanese: そうですう‥‥。
English: Yeah, like, what she said.

Japanese: （この証言‥‥ どうなんだろう‥‥？）
English: (Hmm... Should I drop this line of questioning...?)

Japanese: （こんな細かいところを ゆさぶってもしかたない）
English: (There's no need to press her over something this minor...)

Japanese: （今は、もっと決定的な ムジュンを探さなくちゃ‥‥）
English: (I should be trying to find a more decisive problem with her testimony...)

Japanese: いいですかあ？ 私のお話、つづけても‥‥。
English: So, like, is it OK? I'm going to, like, finish my story, OK?

Japanese: まったく“おかしい”と 思わなかったんですか？
English: So you really didn't think anything "strange" of the whole encounter?

Japanese: ええ。
English: No.

Japanese: ‥‥だってえ、アヤシイって思う 理由がありませんでしたからあ。
English: Like, there wasn't any reason for me to, like, think anything was "strange".

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: あいた！
English: OWW!

Japanese: 同じコトを聞かない。
English: Don't keep badgering her.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうときは“異議あり” って言いましょうよ‥‥
English: ...You know the phrase, "Objection!"? Could you try using that once in a while...?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Hmm... Mr. Wright.

Japanese: “証人がお姉さんを見たとき、 どう思ったか？”‥‥
English: This question of what the witness felt when she saw her sister...

Japanese: ‥‥それが、 そんなに重要なのですか？
English: is it really that important?

Japanese: “重要か重要でないか”‥‥。 はたしてそれが重要なのですか？
English: "Is it really that important?" Let me ask you: Is it really that important for you to ask?

Japanese: もちろん、重要です。
English: Of course it's very important.

Japanese: ぼくはそうでもないと 思います！
English: Well, I don't think it's that important at all!

Japanese: いてぇ！
English: Yeow!

Japanese: ワケのわからないコト 言わない。
English: Stop spewing such nonsensical things then.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。かまわないから 証言をつづけなさい。
English: Pay no attention to this twit and continue, witness.

Japanese: はあい。
English: Like, OK.

Japanese: もちろん、重要です！ （ナニが重要かわからないけど）
English: It is very important! (Although I haven't quite figured out what "it" is yet.)

Japanese: （ここは、ハッタリをカマすべき フンイキと見た‥‥！）
English: (But I've got to bluff for now and make it seem like I know what I'm talking about...)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 証人は、証言を変えてください。
English: Alright, Mr. Wright. Ms. Miney, please amend your testimony.

Japanese: はあい。 いいですけどお、べつにい。
English: Like, OK. I'm, like, totally cool with that.

Japanese: “おそろしいこと”‥‥ って、なんですか？
English: Something "terrible"? What was this thing she told you?

Japanese: あれえ。ついさっき お話ししましたよお？
English: Huh? Like, didn't I just, like, tell you about it?

Japanese: ‥‥もう、 忘れちゃったんですかあ？
English: Did you, like, forget already?

Japanese: バカのバカたるゆえんは バカバカしさゆえコトバにならず。
English: A foolish fool with a foolish sieve for a brain only speaks foolishly with foolish words.

Japanese: （‥‥なんか言ってるよ‥‥。 もう一度、聞くか？）
English: (Well, excuuuse me, Princess. ...Anyway, should I ask to hear the story again...?)

Japanese: （たしかに、さっき 聞いたな‥‥）
English: (I did just hear this story. It's not like my memory is that bad...)

Japanese: ‥‥いえ。やっぱり けっこうです。
English: Actually, the defense is fine and does not need to hear the story again.

Japanese: わかりました。 証言をつづけてもらいましょう。
English: Very well. You may continue your testimony, Ms. Miney.

Japanese: すみませんが、もう一度 聞かせてください。
English: I'm sorry, but could I hear the story one more time?

Japanese: いいですよお。‥‥じゃ、 ムードたっぷりにお話ししますね。
English: Like, sure, no problem. I'll even, like, tell it all spooky-like for you.

Japanese: ‥‥睡眠薬を飲まされたの‥‥
English: That was... no accident...

Japanese: あれは‥‥事故じゃなかったわ
English: I was drugged... with sleeping pills...

Japanese: あたしは‥‥殺されたのよ ‥‥アイツに‥‥
English: I was murdered... by that person...

Japanese: ‥‥だから‥‥復讐してやった‥‥
English: That's why... I took... my revenge...

Japanese: 当然でしょ？　のどか‥‥
English: It's only fair... Isn't it... ...Ini?

Japanese: ‥‥いかがでしたかあ？
English: ...Like, how was that?

Japanese: ちょっと、長かったですね。
English: It was a little scary...

Japanese: すみませえん。 キアイが入っちゃって‥‥
English: Like, I'm sorry. Maybe I, like, put too much spookiness into it...?

Japanese: お姉さんのすがたに、 何か変わったことは‥‥？
English: So, there was nothing odd at all about your sister's appearance?

Japanese: そうですねえ。 ありませんでしたよお。
English: Like, yeah. Nothing was, like, weird.

Japanese: （ん？　‥‥千尋さん、 何かつぶやいたような‥‥）
English: (Hm? ...Mia's talking to herself like she's found something wrong...)

Japanese: ‥‥そんなはず、ない‥‥
English: ...That's impossible...

Japanese: あのお。いいですかあ？ お話をつづけても‥‥。
English: Like, is everything OK? Can I, like, go on...?

Japanese: ‥‥のどかさん。 よく考えたウソでしたね。
English: That was a very well-spun lie, Ms. Ini.

Japanese: な‥‥何を言い出すの！ 成歩堂　龍一！
English: Wh-What are you babbling about this time, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: よくできた作り話です。 でも‥‥それ以上ではない。
English: It's well constructed... but that's all it is.

Japanese: それ‥‥私のセリフじゃない。
English: Stealing MY lines now, are we, Phoenix?

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか、のどかさん。 あなたは、こう証言しました。
English: Ms. Miney, may I remind you of what you said?

Japanese: “お姉さんのすがたを見たとき、 特にあやしいと思わなかった”
English: You said that there was nothing strange about your sister's appearance.

Japanese: そ、そうですけどお‥‥？
English: L-Like, yeah... And...?

Japanese: それはあり得ないんです！ ‥‥この写真を見てください。
English: But I don't really think you thought that at all. Take a look at this picture.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたが会った “お姉さん”の写真です。
English: This is a picture of the "sister" you met.

Japanese: この人物が目の前に現れたら‥‥ とりあえずビックリするでしょう！
English: I would think that even you would be surprised if someone like this appeared before you.

Japanese: ‥‥返り血‥‥
English: The blood spray...

Japanese: のどかさん！
English: Ms. Miney!

Japanese: どうしてあなたは、返り血の ことを証言しなかったのですか！
English: Why did you not include the blood you saw in your testimony!?

Japanese: 本来ならば、まっさきに 証言されるはずです！
English: If you were really testifying, and not lying, you would have noted it right off the bat!

Japanese: ‥‥ううぅっ！
English: Ummm...!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥証人、どうなんですか？
English: Order! Order! Ms. Miney! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: ‥‥証人？
English: ...Ms. Miney?

Japanese: 今、考えてるでしょ！ 話しかけないで！
English: I'm thinking! Don't interrupt me!

Japanese: ‥‥あ、そ、その‥‥
English: ...Um, that...

Japanese: へひッ！
English: Eep!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長がウロタエて どうするの‥‥
English: And what are you stammering about, Your Honor...?

Japanese: え、あ、あ。 そ、そうでした‥‥。
English: Um, uh... Well... I... I uhh...

Japanese: ‥‥あの証人‥‥ 急に性格が変わるのね。
English: That witness... Her personality did a complete 180 there.

Japanese: 証言台に立つと そういう人、多いですよ。
English: Lots of people do that when they get on the stand, though.

Japanese: 証人。‥‥まずは、 落ちつきなさい。
English: First of all, calm yourself down, witness.

Japanese: すみませえん。私、 カッとなっちゃうタチでえ‥‥。
English: Like, I'm sorry. I, like, didn't mean to snap...

Japanese: ‥‥では。 証言をつづけなさい。
English: Now, hurry up with the testimony.

Japanese: おね、おねがいします‥‥。
English: I-If you please...

Japanese: （裁判長‥‥ まだビビってるぞ）
English: (Ugh... The judge still looks a little shaken up... A lot of help he's going to be...)

Japanese: ～事件の直後、目撃したこと・２～
English: -- After the Murder, Pt.2 --

Japanese: “控えの間”って、ちょっと 暗かったんですよお。
English: Like, the Side Room was, like, kinda dark, you know?

Japanese: あの装束、ムラサキだったし、 血が見えなかったんですう。
English: So like, the costume is, like, purple, right? The blood totally blended right in.

Japanese: 私、お姉さんを説得したんですう。 こんなことしちゃダメ、って。
English: And I, like, persuaded my sister it wasn't, like, right to do something like that.

Japanese: そして‥‥“対面の間”へ、 お姉さんをつれて行きましたあ。
English: And then... like, I took my sister to the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ なるほど、わかりました。
English: Hmm... Yes, now I see.

Japanese: 血に気がつかなかった理由も ハッキリしましたな。
English: And you have given us a clear reason for why you didn't notice the blood.

Japanese: そうでしょお？ ありがとうございまあす。
English: You think? Like, thanks, gramps!

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人。
English: Are you satisfied, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ナットクできない、と。
English: Alright, I guess not...

Japanese: わかりました。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Very well. You may question the witness.

Japanese: ～事件の直後、目撃したこと・２～
English: -- After the Murder, Pt.2 --

Japanese: “控えの間”って、ちょっと 暗かったんですよお。
English: Like, the Side Room was, like, kinda dark, you know?

Japanese: あの装束、ムラサキだったし、 血が見えなかったんですう。
English: So like, the costume is, like, purple, right? The blood totally blended right in.

Japanese: 私、お姉さんを説得したんですう。 こんなコトしちゃダメ、って。
English: And I, like, persuaded my sister it wasn't, like, right to do something like that.

Japanese: そして‥‥“対面の間”へ、 お姉さんをつれて行きましたあ。
English: And then... like, I took my sister to the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ。おかしいところ なんて、ないぞ‥‥）
English: (No good. I can't find anything wrong...)

Japanese: なるほどくん。裁判長は この証言にナットクしているわ。
English: Phoenix, the Judge believes this testimony.

Japanese: ここで決定的なムジュンを 見つけないと‥‥
English: If you don't find some sort of hitch in her statement...

Japanese: 審理は終了、ですか‥‥。
English: He'll end the trial... I know...

Japanese: もう一度！ ‥‥私たちに次はないのよ！
English: We have to give it another shot!

Japanese: あきらめずに、何度でも 食いさがりましょう！
English: It doesn't matter how many times we listen. We can't give up!

Japanese: “対面の間”へ行く途中は、 だれにも会いませんでしたあ。
English: I, like, didn't see anyone on the way to, like, the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか？　あの部屋、 かなり明るかったと思いますが。
English: Really? Because I thought it was pretty bright inside that room.

Japanese: あなたのヒトリよがりな 主観を押しつけないで。
English: Don't push your subjective opinions onto this court.

Japanese: そうですよお。
English: Like, that's right!

Japanese: い、いやいや。のどかさんの 証言だって主観でしょう！
English: Um, excuse me, but how is my statement any more subjective than Ms. Miney's?

Japanese: ラチがあかないわね。 ‥‥ほっときなさい、こんな男。
English: You are such a hopeless cause. Witness, you may feel free to ignore this trite little man.

Japanese: はあい。
English: Like, OK.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥セツない‥‥）
English: (Uuugh... Can't breathe... Let me outta here...)

Japanese: 血が‥‥見えなかった？
English: You... didn't see the blood...?

Japanese: はあい。
English: Like, yeah.

Japanese: でも‥‥その人物は、ピストルも ぶら下げていたハズですよ。
English: But she must have had the gun with her at that time as well.
That's um...

Japanese: ピストルにも気がつかなかったと 言うつもりですか！
English: Are you going to tell me next that you didn't notice the gun either!?

Japanese: き、気がつきませんでしたあ。 カオばっかり見てたから‥‥。
English: I, like, totally didn't. All I was looking at was, like, her face...

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 証言をつづけなさい。
English: Continue.

Japanese: お姉さん‥‥葉中　未実さんの 反応はどうでしたか？
English: And what was your sister, Ms. Mimi Miney, like at the time?

Japanese: 霧崎先生を撃っちゃって、 スゴくコーフンしてましたケド‥‥
English: Well, she was, like, totally flipped out 'cause she just shot Dr. Grey...

Japanese: わかってくれたみたいです。 申しわけないコトをした、って。
English: But, I think, like, she knew. She, like, said she did something really bad.

Japanese: 私といっしょに、キミ子さんに あやまりに行こう、って。
English: And, like, she said she wanted to go with me to, like, apologize to Ms. Morgan.

Japanese: そんなコトを言ったんですか？ 未実さんの霊が‥‥。
English: Did Mimi's spirit really say all that to you...?

Japanese: そうなんですよお‥‥。
English: Like, yeah...

Japanese: そのとき、 お姉さんはおとなしく‥‥？
English: Was your sister calm by that time?

Japanese: そうですねえ‥‥。
English: Like, I guess so...

Japanese: たぶん、霧崎先生に復讐して、 スッキリしたんじゃないですかあ？
English: I guess maybe, like, taking her revenge on Dr. Grey, like, made her feel a lot better...

Japanese: 私も早くスッキリしたいものね。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: I'd like to feel a lot better too... Mr. Phoenix Wright. The sooner the better...

Japanese: （ムチをかまえて 言われてもなあ‥‥）
English: (She says with her whip at the ready...)

Japanese: “対面の間”には、キミ子さん しかいませんでしたあ。
English: And, like, Ms. Morgan was the only one in the Channeling Chamber, you know?

Japanese: ‥‥もう１つ、いいですか？ のどかさん。
English: May I ask you one more thing, Ms. Miney?

Japanese: なんですかあ？
English: Like, sure.

Japanese: 何か、変わったことは ありませんでしたか？
English: Was there anything you found strange, unusual, or just out of place?

Japanese: 変わったコト‥‥？ あの、いつですかあ？
English: Something out of place? Huh? Like, when do you mean?

Japanese: い、いつ、って‥‥
English: Uh, when...? Um... When you...

Japanese: もちろん、“控えの間”に いたときですよ！
English: I'm talking about when you were in the Side Room, of course!

Japanese: そうですねえ‥‥ 特にありませんでしたよお。
English: Um... Like, no, nothing strange.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥ どうしよう‥‥）
English: (Hmm... What should I do...?)

Japanese: よく思い出してください！
English: Think harder! I know you can remember something!

Japanese: よく思い出せませえん。
English: I, like, totally can't!

Japanese: じゃ、しかたないわね。 あきらめなさい、成歩堂　龍一。
English: Just give up already, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: （‥‥この方向は まちがっていたのかな‥‥）
English: (I wonder if I put too much faith in Ini's ability to do anything with that brain...)

Japanese: （どうやら、決定的な ムジュンを探さないかぎり）
English: (Even if I push her, this is probably a dead end...)

Japanese: （ゆさぶってもムダ みたいだな‥‥）
English: (I should search for something stronger to hook her with.)

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥では、証人。 証言にもどってください。
English: ...Hmm... Well, if the defense is finished, please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: はあい。
English: Like, OK.

Japanese: もちろん、“対面の間”に ついたときですよ！
English: I'm talking about when you arrived at the Channeling Chamber, of course!

Japanese: そうですねえ‥‥ 特にありませんでしたよお。
English: Um... Like, no, nothing strange.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥　どうしよう‥‥）
English: (Hmm... What should I do...?)

Japanese: もちろん、“対面の間”に 向かう途中ですよ！
English: I'm talking about when you were going to the Channeling Chamber, of course!

Japanese: そうですねえ‥‥ 特にありませんでしたよお。
English: Um... Like, no, nothing strange.

Japanese: （あっさりかわされた‥‥ どうしよう‥‥）
English: (She answered rather quickly... Now what...?)

Japanese: よく思い出してください！
English: Think harder! I know you can remember something!

Japanese: よく思い出せませえん。
English: I, like, totally can't!

Japanese: そうはいきません！ ここはハッキリさせておきたい！
English: That's not good enough! You have to explain yourself clearly to this court!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 証人がお姉さんをつれて行く途中、 変わったことがなかった‥‥。
English: Do you think, Mr. Wright, that whether or not there was

Japanese: それが、重要なことなのですか？
English: something odd on the way to the Channeling Chamber is all that important?

Japanese: ‥‥正直なところ、 自信はありません！
English: ...Actually, I really have no idea!

Japanese: 自信マンマンに言わないように！
English: Then don't say it like you know what you're talking about!

Japanese: 壮大なスケールで 時間をムダにしたわね‥‥。
English: This has been the most extravagant waste of time that I've ever seen.

Japanese: では、証言を つづけてもらいましょう。
English: Let's continue on with the testimony.

Japanese: 重要だから聞いてるんですよ！
English: I ask because it is VERY important!

Japanese: （‥‥というか、ここまで来て アトにはひけない！）
English: (...Not that I know where I'm going with this...)

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: では、証人。今の発言を、 証言に追加してください。
English: Ms. Miney, if you will, please include a statement about this in your testimony.

Japanese: 別に、かまいませんけどお‥‥。
English: Like, I don't get why, but, OK.

Japanese: よく思い出してください！
English: Try to remember that time!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。 思い出しましたあ。
English: Oh! Like, I remember!

Japanese: やっぱり、だれにも 会ってませえん。
English: I remember, like, I totally didn't see anyone on the way.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥ムダ だったのかな、やっぱり）
English: (Hmm... I guess this really is a dead end...)

Japanese: やっと引きずり出した証言‥‥。 ムダということはないハズよ。
English: Something you finally pull out of a witness is never a dead end, Phoenix.

Japanese: “対面の間”に行く途中、 だれにも会わなかった‥‥
English: You say that you didn't see anyone on the way to the Channeling Chamber...

Japanese: そんなことは あり得ないんですよ！
English: However, that is impossible!

Japanese: ‥‥どういうコト‥‥ ですかあ？
English: ...Like, what do you mean?...

Japanese: ‥‥ご紹介しましょう。
English: I'd like to introduce someone to you.

Japanese: キミ子さんのムスメさん、 春美ちゃんです。
English: This is Ms. Morgan Fey's daughter, Pearl Fey.

Japanese: あなたより、リコウそうなカオね。 ‥‥その子がどうかして？
English: Interesting, you have a semi- intelligent look on your face. So? What about this child?

Japanese: 事件があったとき、春美ちゃんは 庭であそんでいました。
English: It just so happens that at the time of the murder, Pearl was playing in the center garden.

Japanese: 庭で‥‥？
English: The garden...?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: Yup!

Japanese: あの時間、わたりろうかを通れば かならず、見かけたハズです！
English: Which means anyone walking through the area would have to have seen her!

Japanese: さあ、のどかさん。 どうですかッ！
English: So, Ms. Miney, what do you have to say to that!?

Japanese: うぐぐウゥッ！
English: Nnaaawrr!

Japanese: ‥‥見てのとおり、 この証人は少々、ノンビリ屋さん。
English: As the court can see... she's a bit of an airhead.

Japanese: しかも、そのとき彼女は “姉”をつれて歩いていた。
English: Also, she was leading her "sister" at that time.

Japanese: ‥‥ちっちゃくてジミな子供に 気づかないのは、しごく当然！
English: Do you honestly think she would have noticed a simple child playing!? Preposterous!

Japanese: そうはいかないんですよ。 ‥‥証拠があります！
English: It's not preposterous, and I can prove it!

Japanese: な‥‥何をバカな‥‥
English: Wh-What sort of foolish...

Japanese: わたりろうかを通って、春美ちゃん を見かけないハズがないんです！
English: There is no way someone walking along the Winding Way could not have noticed Pearl!

Japanese: ‥‥いや。ちがうな。
English: Oops, silly me. Slip of the finger.

Japanese: べべ、弁護人。せめて答えを 言ってからハンセイしてください。
English: M-Mr. Wright. You need to keep control of that finger of yours'.

Japanese: ‥‥さすがにこれはちがう、 って思ったもので。
English: ...As I thought, this is the wrong piece of evidence.

Japanese: いてぇ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: そんなイイカゲンなキモチの弁護人 などいらない。帰りなさい！
English: An irresponsible lawyer has no place in this court! I suggest you go home!

Japanese: （うう‥‥“帰れ”と来たか‥‥）
English: (Oww... So it's come down to a blunt "go home"...)

Japanese: むずかしく考えないで。 きのうのことを思い出すの！
English: Don't over-think this. All you need to do is remember what happened yesterday.

Japanese: ‥‥その証拠は、 もちろんこのツボです。
English: This urn is my proof.

Japanese: その、ヒビだらけの ムサいツボがどうかして？
English: An old, cracked clay pot? What is that going to prove?

Japanese: なぜ、ヒビだらけなのか？ 春美ちゃんが割ったからです！
English: Do you know why it's cracked? It's because Pearl broke it! That's why!

Japanese: ツボを‥‥割った？
English: She broke the urn...?

Japanese: まはッ！
English: Yeow!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだか イヤな予感がする‥‥。
English: Why... Why do I feel like you're about to ruin my beautiful day...?

Japanese: ‥‥ツボが割れたのは、 霊媒が始まったころのことです！
English: This urn was broken around the time the channeling started!

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃんは そいつを修理していたんですよ。
English: And Pearl was the one who put it back together...

Japanese: ‥‥わたりろうかに座りこんで！
English: ...while sitting right in the middle of the Winding Way!

Japanese: なんですってェェェェッ！
English: WH-WHAAAAAAAAAAAAAT!?

Japanese: のどかさん。ついに‥‥ 決定的なボロを出しましたね。
English: I've finally found the fault, or I guess in your case, the "crack" in your perfect lie.

Japanese: このツボを見てください。
English: Take a look at this urn.

Japanese: ツボ‥‥？
English: Like, urn...?

Japanese: ヒビが入っています。 ‥‥気がつきませんでしたか？
English: There are cracks. Do you see them?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、 それがどうしたんですかあ？
English: S-So? Like, what does that prove?

Japanese: そんなみすぼらしいツボ、 どうでもいいわ！
English: Who cares about some worn-out old urn!?

Japanese: そうですよお。
English: Like, yeah.

Japanese: 私、現場に行く途中、だれにも 会わなかったって言っただけです。
English: I already, like, said I didn't see, like, anyone on the way to the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: ツボのコトなんて、 何も言ってませんよお？
English: I didn't, like, totally say anything about, like, an urn, did I?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、まだ わからないようですね。
English: It looks like you still don't understand.

Japanese: もし本当にあの時間、 わたりろうかを通ったのなら、
English: I propose that if you had really been walking along the Winding Way at that time,

Japanese: あなたはこの人物を 見ていなければおかしい！
English: there was no way you could not have seen this person!

Japanese: ‥‥いや。ちがうな。
English: Oops, silly me. Slip of the finger.

Japanese: べべ、弁護人。せめて答えを 言ってからハンセイしてください。
English: M-Mr. Wright. You need to keep control of that finger of yours'.

Japanese: ‥‥さすがにこれはちがう、 って思ったもので。
English: ...As I thought, this is the wrong piece of evidence.

Japanese: いてぇ！
English: Oww!

Japanese: そんなイイカゲンなキモチの弁護人 などいらない。帰りなさい！
English: An irresponsible lawyer has no place in this court! I suggest you go home!

Japanese: （うう‥‥“帰れ”と来たか‥‥）
English: (Oww... So it's come down to a blunt "go home"...)

Japanese: むずかしく考えないで。 きのうのことを思い出すの！
English: Don't over-think this. All you need to do is remember what happened yesterday.

Japanese: ‥‥ご紹介しましょう。
English: I'd like to introduce someone to you.

Japanese: キミ子さんのムスメさん、 春美ちゃんです。
English: This is Ms. Morgan Fey's daughter, Pearl Fey.

Japanese: そ‥‥その子が、何か‥‥？
English: Y-Yes...? And how is this child relevant...?

Japanese: 霊媒が始まったころ、春美ちゃんは 庭であそんでいました。
English: It just so happens that at the time of the murder, Pearl was playing in the center garden.

Japanese: ほお‥‥
English: Oh...

Japanese: そして‥‥割ってしまったんです。 このツボを、ね。
English: And she just happened to break this urn.

Japanese: ほお‥‥
English: Oh...

Japanese: ひぐッ！
English: OHHH!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ちなさい！ ‥‥ツボを‥‥割った？
English: W-Wait! She... broke the urn...?

Japanese: そのとおり！ マリをぶつけてね！
English: Yup. She accidentally knocked it over with her ball!

Japanese: う‥‥ッ！
English: Nnaaawrr!

Japanese: そして、春美ちゃんは そいつを修理していたんですよ。
English: And Pearl was the one who put it back together...

Japanese: ‥‥わたりろうかに座りこんで！
English: ...while sitting right in the middle of the Winding Way!

Japanese: なんですってェェェェッ！
English: WH-WHAAAAAAAAAAAAAT!?

Japanese: のどかさん‥‥。
English: Ms. Miney.

Japanese: 春美ちゃんは、あの時間、 わたりろうかにいたんですよ。
English: Pearl was there, in the Winding Way at that time.

Japanese: ツボのカケラをろうか中に ぶちまけて、ね。
English: She was hard at work, putting the pieces of the urn back together, you see.

Japanese: それを見落とすことは ゼッタイ、不可能です！
English: If you couldn't see that while you were walking, I'd have to declare you legally blind!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ぐぐッ‥‥
English: G...Grrrrr...

Japanese: つまり、のどかさん。 ‥‥これで証明されたわけです。
English: I think I have sufficiently proven one thing, Ms. Miney.

Japanese: あなたは、大ウソつきだ！
English: You are a masterful liar!

Japanese: ぐぐううウッ！
English: Arghnnn!

Japanese: 弁護人！ 口をつつしみなさい！
English: Mr. Wright! You need to watch what you say!

Japanese: 口をつつしむのは そこの証人です！
English: The one who needs to watch what she's saying is the witness!

Japanese: さあ、のどかさん！ 教えてもらいましょうか！
English: So, Ms. Miney! Tell us the truth!

Japanese: な、何を‥‥！
English: A-About what!?

Japanese: 決まっているでしょう！
English: About what!?

Japanese: 事件があったとき、あなたが 本当はどこにいたのか！
English: About where you really were at the time of the murder, of course!

Japanese: それはすでに 証言されているはずよ！
English: The witness just testified about that!

Japanese: そ、そうよ！
English: Th-That's right!

Japanese: 私は、“控えの間”で 寝ていた、って‥‥
English: I was, like, sleeping in the Side Room...

Japanese: （‥‥“控えの間”で寝ていた！ 本当にそうなのか‥‥？）
English: (...Can I really believe her? Was she really sleeping in the Side Room...!?)

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね‥‥。 それは問題ないと思います。
English: Yeah, I guess so. Sorry, my mistake.

Japanese: わかりました。 では、先に進みましょう‥‥
English: Very well. Now, then, let's proceed.

Japanese: ‥‥待って！ なるほどくん。
English: Wait! Phoenix!

Japanese: ま、またやっちゃいました？ ミスを‥‥。
English: Wh-What? Did I miss something again?

Japanese: あの証人が“控えの間”に いたことを認めちゃダメよ！
English: You shouldn't let up on this line of questioning.

Japanese: え‥‥ どうしてですか？
English: Huh? Why not?

Japanese: 霧崎先生が殺害された瞬間、 あなたが見ていない人物はひとり。
English: Who is the only person you didn't see with your own eyes at the time of the murder?

Japanese: あの証人、葉中　のどかだけ。
English: It was Ini Miney, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥ということは、 つまり。犯人はあの証人よ。
English: Which means, you can safely assume...
She is the real murderer.

Japanese: ‥‥ダイタンな推理ですね。
English: That's a pretty daring assumption...

Japanese: わかりやすいでしょ。 だって、他にいないんだから。
English: But it's the most obvious, isn't it? I mean, is there anyone else?

Japanese: ええ、まあ。
English: Well, no, I guess not.

Japanese: ハッタリでもなんでもいいわ。
English: It's worth a shot, even if it's in the dark.

Japanese: あの証人が《控えの間》に いなかったことを証明するの！
English: So, let's try to prove that Ms. Miney was not in the Side Room at the time!

Japanese: （‥‥これで何度目だ？ この事件でかますハッタリは）
English: (Umm, this is bluff number...? We've done this so many times in this case I've lost count.)

Japanese: そんな主張は、もう通らない！
English: Your lies end here!

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうコトよ！
English: Wh-What do you!?

Japanese: 証人は、事件のあいだ ずっと“控えの間”で寝ていた‥‥
English: The witness says that during the actual murder, she was asleep in the Side Room.

Japanese: それはあり得ない。 ‥‥ムジュンしています！
English: And I say that's not possible, because there is clearly a contradiction here!

Japanese: ムジュン‥‥！　いったい、 何とムジュンしているんですか！
English: A contradiction...!? Where is this contradiction!?

Japanese: もちろん、答えは 法廷記録の中にあります！
English: The answer is in the Court Record!

Japanese: な、なんと！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: またワケのわからない 証拠品が飛びかうワケね‥‥。
English: Hmph. Planning to throw out another irrelevant piece of evidence?

Japanese: では、さっそく提示して もらいましょう‥‥
English: Very well. The court will see this evidence...

Japanese: 待って！
English: Wait!

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さん！
English: M-Mia!

Japanese: あなたが何を見せようと しているのか知らないけど、
English: I don't know what you plan on showing to the court,

Japanese: 答えはその中にはないわ。
English: but I can guarantee you the answer is not there.

Japanese: 今回だけは助けてあげる。 ‥‥考えなおしなさい。
English: Just this once, I'll lend you a hand. Now, rethink your answer.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか‥‥？
English: A-Alright...

Japanese: （イキオイで言ってしまった ものの‥‥）
English: (Hmm, I may have overreached on this one...)

Japanese: （ホントにあるのかな？ ムジュンなんて‥‥）
English: (Is there really a contradiction somewhere...?)

Japanese: ‥‥だいじょうぶよ。
English: Don't worry, you're on the right path, Phoenix.

Japanese: そうね。‥‥あなたには ちょっと、ムズカシイかもね。
English: It might be a little hard for you to see the contradiction, but it's there.

Japanese: 今回だけは‥‥ 教えてあげましょうか？
English: So, just this once, do you want me to help you out?

Japanese: 教えてください！ さあ。今すぐに！
English: Please, I need your help right now!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょ、ちょっとは まよってほしかったわね。
English: H-Hold on... First, calm down, Phoenix.

Japanese: ‥‥いいわ。
English: ...Good.

Japanese: ‥‥彼女は、そう‥‥。 口をすべらせてしまったの。
English: ...That girl... She slipped up with her words.
...Her words?

Japanese: もし彼女がずっと“控えの間”に いたのなら、
English: If she really was in the Side Room the entire time,

Japanese: 知っているはずのないことを、 彼女は証言しているの。
English: then she just testified about something she shouldn't have any knowledge of.

Japanese: い、いったいそれは‥‥！
English: A-And what is that!?

Japanese: ‥‥そこから先は、 自分で考えなさい。
English: The rest is for you to figure out on your own.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。 そろそろ答えを聞かせてください。
English: Mr. Wright. What is your answer?

Japanese: （くそ！　もう一度 ハッタリをかますか！）
English: (Argh! OK, time to add another bluff to the pile...)

Japanese: （“あなたには”ムズカシイ ‥‥そんなことを言われて）
English: (Hmm, it's "hard for me to see"...?)

Japanese: （教えてもらうわけには 行かない！）
English: (I can't just accept her help without thinking it through myself!)

Japanese: ふふ‥‥。 それなら、考えてみて。
English: Ha ha, that's the spirit. Give it another try.

Japanese: ‥‥さっきの尋問で、のどかさんは こんな証言をしました。
English: Earlier in your testimony, Ms. Miney, you made the following statement.

Japanese: “対面の間”には、キミ子さん しかいませんでしたあ。
English: And, like, Ms. Morgan was the only one in the Channeling Chamber, you know?

Japanese: ‥‥たしかにあのとき、 ナツミさんとぼくは、いなかった。
English: It's true that at the time, both myself and Ms. Hart were not there.

Japanese: 警察へ通報しに 行ってましたからね。
English: We had gone to call the police.

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それが なんだって言うのよ！
English: ...S-So? Like, what does that, like, have to do with anything!?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなことです。
English: It's very simple.

Japanese: どうしてそれを あなたが知っているんですか？
English: Ms. Miney, how did you know that fact?

Japanese: たしかに“対面の間”には キミ子さんしかいませんでした。
English: I concede that Ms. Fey was the only person in the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: しかし、その情報は“控えの間”に 寝ていては、知ることはできない！
English: However, someone who was "asleep" in the Side Room could not have known that!

Japanese: つまり、のどかさん！
English: Which means, Ms. Miney!

Japanese: あなたは、“対面の間”に 行ったことになる！
English: You did, in fact, go to the Channeling Chamber.

Japanese: それも、 わたりろうかを通らずに！
English: However, you didn't go there by way of the Winding Way!

Japanese: ぐはああァァッ‥‥ッ！
English: Gwaaaaaaaaaaaa!

Japanese: し‥‥しかし！ お屋敷の上面図を見てください！
English: B-But! Look at the manor guidemap!

Japanese: “控えの間”から“対面の間”へ 行くためには、
English: If one were to go between the Side Room and the Channeling Chamber,

Japanese: わたりろうかを通るしか ありません！
English: one would have to use the Winding Way!

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。 だからこそ‥‥
English: Yes, that's right, Your Honor. Which is why...

Japanese: だからこそ、聞いているんです！
English: Which is why I ask this very important question!

Japanese: 事件当時、 証人はどこにいたのか、と！
English: Where was the witness at the time of the murder!?
...Umm... Urrrr...

Japanese: のどかさん！　答えてください！
English: Ms. Miney! Please answer the question!

Japanese: コンキョのない推理で 尋問をしないでほしいわね‥‥
English: Don't ask a question off of a baseless assumption, if you please...

Japanese: そこまで言うのなら ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: Well, I suppose since you've put it out on the table, Mr. Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: あなたの考えを言いなさい！
English: you might as well answer the question for us!

Japanese: “事件があったとき、 証人がどこにいたのか”！
English: "Where was the witness at the time of the murder!?"

Japanese: チャンスよ！　なるほどくん。
English: Now's your chance, Phoenix.

Japanese: はい！
English: Yeah!

Japanese: 霧崎先生を殺害できたのは 葉中　のどかしかいない！
English: The only person who could have killed Dr. Grey was Ini Miney!

Japanese: ‥‥今こそ、 それを証明してやりましょうッ！
English: So now is the time to prove it!

Japanese: では、弁護人。あなたの 考えを示してください。
English: Now, then, Mr. Wright. Let's hear your answer.

Japanese: 事件が発生したとき、 証人はどこにいたのか‥‥？
English: Where was the witness when the murder took place...?

Japanese: それは、ここ‥‥。
English: She was here...

Japanese: ちょっと！ ナニ言ってるのなるほどくん！
English: Phoenix! Did you turn the map upside down!?

Japanese: （‥‥いてて。手を ピシャリとたたかれた‥‥）
English: (...Oww... Sh-She slapped me on the wrist...)

Japanese: そんなところにいたら、 犯行はムリじゃない！
English: There is no way she could've committed the crime from there!

Japanese: コラ、そこ！
English: *ahem* You two!

Japanese: ‥‥ケンカなら、審理が 終わってからにするように。
English: Keep disrupting this court, and this trial is over. Is that clear!?

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥。 もう一度おねがいします。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor... Sorry...

Japanese: もちろん、のどかさんは ここにいたんですよ。
English: Ms. Miney was here, of course!

Japanese: な！　そ‥‥そこは “対面の間”‥‥
English: Wha... But... But that's... the Channeling Chamber...

Japanese: ‥‥事件のあった現場じゃないッ！
English: Isn't that the crime scene!?

Japanese: そのとおり！
English: That's right!

Japanese: のどかさんは 殺人現場にいたんです！
English: Ms. Miney was at the scene of the crime!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥なんともその、あー、ええ‥‥
English: Order! Order! Order! ...That's... Why, that is...

Japanese: はむッ！
English: Uwaah!

Japanese: ナニを言うの、成歩堂　龍一！ きのうの証言を思い出しなさい！
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright! Have you lost your mind!? Yesterday's testimony established that

Japanese: 霊媒が始まったとき、あの部屋には 被告と被害者しかいなかったはず！
English: only the victim and the defendant were in the Chamber when the channeling started!

Japanese: そう！　それです！ ‥‥どうなんですか弁護人！
English: Yes! Yes, that is correct! Please, explain yourself, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: もちろん、のどかさんは かくれていたんです。現場に。
English: Simple. Ms. Ini Miney was hiding at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: あたしが‥‥ かくれていたですって！
English: I... I was hiding!?

Japanese: どこに！　現場のどこに かくれるところがあったのよッ！
English: Where!? Like, where was I hiding!?

Japanese: のどかさんは、ここに かくれていたんです！
English: Ms. Miney was hiding here!

Japanese: いてっ！
English: Owww!

Japanese: ジョークを言うなら、 せめて笑わせてほしいわね！
English: If you're going to tell a joke, at least make it one I'll laugh at.

Japanese: はん！　とっととウチに帰って、 “かくれる”の意味を調べればあ！
English: Hah! Go home, boy, and look up what "hiding" means!

Japanese: （くそ‥‥こっちもテンションを 上げて行かなければ‥‥！）
English: (Argh... Looks like I'm going to have to one up her!)

Japanese: ああ！　帰って調べてやるともさ！
English: Yeah, count on it!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっとなるほどくん。 ヘンにエキサイトしないで！
English: Phoenix, you can't lose your cool like this! ...And, grow up.

Japanese: え。‥‥あ、す、すみません。
English: Eh?... Oh, um, sorry, Chief...

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。 まだ、コリてないみたいね！
English: You still haven't learned your lesson, have you, Mr. Phoenix Wright!?

Japanese: それは、もちろんここです。
English: Here, of course.

Japanese: ビョウブの‥‥ウラ、ですか‥‥
English: B-Behind the folding screen?

Japanese: はふッ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: 何度も言わせないで！ きのうの証言を思い出しなさい！
English: Don't make me say this again! Recall yesterday's testimony!

Japanese: ビョウブのウラ、ヒトなんか おらんかったハズやで！
English: There ain't no way anyone was hangin' out behind that foldin' screen!

Japanese: ほら見なさい！ バカじゃないの、アホ！
English: See! You stupid jerk!

Japanese: アセってソンしたじゃない！
English: Quit being so quick to pin it on me, slimeball!

Japanese: ‥‥ま、まあまあ、証人。 ワカい女性がそんな‥‥
English: ...N-Now, now... Ms. Miney... One so young as you shouldn't be saying...

Japanese: ナニよ！　イイ年して、 アンタがキチンとしろっての！
English: Shut it, gramps. As if you know exactly how old I am!

Japanese: ス、スミマセン！
English: I-I'm sorry!

Japanese: （もうゲンカイだな。 そろそろキメてやるか‥‥）
English: (Looks like the pipe's about to burst... Guess I should help it along...)

Japanese: 証人は、ビョウブのウラに かくれていた。‥‥これを使って！
English: The witness was hiding behind the folding screen... With the help of this!

Japanese: アンタがかくれてればぁ！ それを使って。
English: Yeah, you go ahead and use that to hide!

Japanese: そして、死ぬまで出てくるな！
English: And you should hide in shame until you die!

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、あのふたり、 ミョーに息が合ってきたな‥‥）
English: (What's with these two...? It's like they did a mind meld all of a sudden...)

Japanese: 感心してちゃダメでしょ！ すぐ考えなおして。
English: Don't let them affect you! Just think about the correct answer.

Japanese: どーなのよコラ！ なんとか言いなさいよ！
English: What's wrong, boy!? Come on, say something, you loser!

Japanese: のどかさん！
English: Ms. Miney!

Japanese: あなたが、どうやって 現場にかくれていたか‥‥
English: How you were able to hide at the scene of the crime is very simple!

Japanese: カンタンなコトです。 ‥‥この中に入っていた！
English: You were hiding inside this box!

Japanese: うう‥‥ッ！
English: Aaah!

Japanese: ‥‥そそ、そ、そんな小さなハコ、 ヒトなんか入れない‥‥
English: Th-Th-That tiny little box!? No person could fit in that!

Japanese: ザンネンながら、 すでにもう実験してあります！
English: Sorry, but your theory has already been disproved.

Japanese: （ナツミさんが 入れるのはわかってるからな）
English: (Lotta disproved you when she was hiding from me in it.)

Japanese: う‥‥ッ！
English: Umm...!

Japanese: その衣装ばこはたしか、 “控えの間”にあったものでしょ！
English: But that clothing box was in the Side Room, right!?

Japanese: この事件とは関係ないわ！
English: Which means it has nothing to do with the murder at all!

Japanese: ‥‥そうよ！　あそこで 寝てたから、私、知ってるわ。
English: Th-That's right! I was sleeping there, so I should know!

Japanese: 衣装ばこなら、ずっとあったわよ！ “控えの間”にね！
English: That clothing box was there in the Side Room, the whole time!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥ 成歩堂くん、どうなんですか？
English: Hmm... Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 事件の際、その衣装ばこが どこにあったか、証明できますか？
English: Do you think you can prove where the clothing box was at the time of the crime?

Japanese: そうですね。 証明はムズカシイでしょう。
English: Hmm... It's kind of hard to prove...

Japanese: あ、あの。‥‥もしかして、 証明できるんですか？
English: Um... I guess by that look, there is a way to prove it?

Japanese: 法廷記録をちょっと見ればね。
English: If you look in the Court Record.

Japanese: （ちょっと、見てみるか ‥‥法廷記録）
English: (Guess I should take a look at the Court Record...)

Japanese: では、これで‥‥
English: Well, then. If there are...

Japanese: おま、お待ちください‥‥
English: P-Please wait...

Japanese: “ムズカシイ”とは言いましたが、 “証明できない”とは言ってない！
English: I said it was hard, but I didn't say it was impossible to prove!

Japanese: じゃあ、イバってないで 早くしなさい！
English: Then stop puffing up your feathers and hurry up!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、提示しましょう。
English: And now, I present the piece of evidence that will prove

Japanese: 事件の際、衣装ばこが 殺人現場にあった、という証拠を！
English: this clothing box was at the scene of the crime when the murder took place!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。ナニを‥‥？
English: ...Um... Your Honor...?

Japanese: 寝たフリ‥‥です。
English: I... um, fell asleep...

Japanese: チャンスよ！　なるほどくん。 もう一度、考えるの！
English: You got lucky, Phoenix! Now give it another shot!
A-Alright...

Japanese: ‥‥この衣装ばこを よく見てください。
English: Take a look at this clothing box.

Japanese: バカバカしい。そんなものに 証拠が残ってるわけが‥‥
English: You're soooo stupid. As if there's any sort of evidence in that old thing.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ こ、これは‥‥いったい‥‥！
English: Th-This... What on earth...!?

Japanese: もふッ！
English: Mfph!

Japanese: おどろいてるヒマがあったら さっさと言う！
English: I don't have time for you to sit there surprised. Hurry up and say it already!

Japanese: あ‥‥穴があいています！ 足もとから２０ｃｍの高さに！
English: Th-There's a hole! About 8 inches off the ground!

Japanese: 足もとから２０ｃｍの穴。 ‥‥聞いたことのあるデータです。
English: Hmm, a hole about 8 inches off the ground. Where have we heard that before...?

Japanese: ‥‥ビョウブに‥‥ あいた穴と、同じ高さ！
English: ...The folding screen... It had a hole at the same height...

Japanese: そのとおり！ これで明らかでしょう！
English: That's right! I hope this has opened your eyes to what happened!

Japanese: 発砲があったとき、衣装ばこは ビョウブの近くに置かれていた！
English: At the time of the shooting, the clothing box was sitting behind the folding screen.

Japanese: そして‥‥ピストルの弾丸で つらぬかれたんです！
English: Which is why the bullet from the pistol hit both the box and the folding screen!

Japanese: ビョウブといっしょに！
English: It went through the screen, and then into the box.

Japanese: ‥‥むうウゥゥッ！
English: ...Nnngh...!

Japanese: のどかさん！
English: Ms. Miney!

Japanese: あなたは、ビョウブのウラで チャンスを待っていた！
English: You were hiding behind the folding screen, waiting for your chance!

Japanese: ‥‥そう！　霧崎先生を 殺害するチャンスをッ！
English: Yes, for your chance to kill Dr. Grey!

Japanese: ‥‥うがあああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥それはつまり、こ
English: Order! Order! ...So what you are saying --

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！ この写真は！
English: Th-Then... Mr. Phoenix Wright! What about this picture!?

Japanese: この写真の人物が‥‥
English: Are you saying that the person in this photo...

Japanese: ‥‥葉中　のどかだと言うのッ！
English: is Ms. Ini Miney!?

Japanese: 葉中　のどかだと言うのです！
English: That is exactly what I am saying!

Japanese: 葉中　のどかさん！
English: Ms. Ini Miney!

Japanese: あなたはこの衣装ばこの 中にかくれていた！
English: You were hiding inside this clothing box all along!

Japanese: 霊媒師の装束を着て‥‥ 綾里　真宵に変装していたのです！
English: And you wore a medium's costume to masquerade as Maya Fey!

Japanese: あ‥‥綾里　真宵に変装！
English: T-To masquerade as Maya Fey!?

Japanese: はじめから 計画されていたんですよ。
English: She had it planned from the very beginning!

Japanese: 霧崎先生を殺害して、 被告に罪を着せるところまで！
English: She would kill Dr. Grey, and pin the blame on my client!

Japanese: ‥‥や‥‥やめて‥‥ も、もう‥‥
‥‥ダマって聞いていれば バカなコトをぐだぐだと‥‥！
English: ...I can't stand to listen to any more of this foolishness!

Japanese: それならば、成歩堂　龍一！ あなたはこう主張するの！
English: If that's the case, Mr. Phoenix Wright, then I have a proposition!

Japanese: この証人が、現場に衣装ばこを 運びこみ、被告に変装して‥‥、
English: This whole idea that the witness moved the clothing box to the crime scene,

Japanese: 被害者を殺害したあと、 現場を脱出した、と！
English: pretended to be the defendant, killed the victim, and then fled the scene of the crime;

Japanese: ‥‥たった１人でそんなコト、 不可能に決まってるでしょう！
English: It's not possible for one person to do all that by herself!

Japanese: やればできたハズです！
English: I'm sure it's possible if you were to try!

Japanese: いてっ！
English: Ow!

Japanese: “ハズ”とか、そんなことを 聞いてるんじゃない！
English: I don't want to hear "if"!

Japanese: それなら立証してみなさい！
English: If you're going to give me "if", then give me some proof along with it.

Japanese: 葉中　のどかは、どこで装束を 手に入れたの！　カツラは！
English: Where did Ini Miney get the costume then!? How about the wig!?

Japanese: い‥‥いや、ぼくが言いたいのは そういうコトじゃない。
English: Uh, I wasn't talking about that exact point...

Japanese: 人間、ヤル気さえ あればですね‥‥
English: I meant, people, humans. If we want to do something bad enough, then...

Japanese: もうよろしい！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: “１人で犯行が可能だった” と主張する以上‥‥、
English: Beyond simply proposing that it is possible for one person to commit this crime,

Japanese: 弁護人には、それを 立証するギムがあります。
English: it is the duty of a lawyer to back up his or her claims.

Japanese: ギムがあんのよ！
English: Yeah, it's your duty!

Japanese: それができなければ、 主張は通りません。
English: If you can't do that, then your proposal falls flat.

Japanese: 通らないっツってんのよ！
English: Yeah, it totally falls flat!

Japanese: （く‥‥くやしい‥‥）
English: (...Why... Why do you have to hurt me so...?)

Japanese: だいじょうぶよ。 ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: It's alright, Phoenix.
Huh?

Japanese: たしかに、１人ではムリ。 でも‥‥
English: It isn't possible for one person to commit this crime, but...

Japanese: （‥‥あ！　そ、そうだった！）
English: Ack! That's... That's right!)

Japanese: たしかに、不可能でしょう。
English: It really is impossible.

Japanese: ‥‥犯行の準備を ひとりでするのは、ね。
English: For one person to do all the preparations, that is.

Japanese: いてててっ！
English: Owww!

Japanese: だから言ったでしょう！ バカだからってヨウシャは‥‥
English: Need I remind you!? The foolish receive no mercy...

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと 待ってください！
English: W-Wait!

Japanese: 狩魔検事。‥‥“ひとりではムリ” と言ったんですよ？
English: Ms. von Karma... You said it was not possible for "one person", correct?

Japanese: な‥‥！　ま、まさか‥‥
English: Wh--! Y-You can't be serious...

Japanese: ‥‥ハァ‥‥ハァ‥‥
のどかさん！
English: Ms. Miney!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたには、 共犯者がいたんですよ！
English: You had an accomplice!

Japanese: ‥‥この人物が、共犯者‥‥。 いかがですか？　狩魔検事。
English: The accomplice was...?? Any thoughts, Ms. von Karma?

Japanese: ムチをふる気にもなれない。 そちらでテキトーにサバいて。
English: I don't even want to waste the energy to raise my whip. His lady friend can deal with him.

Japanese: もう！ 狩魔検事もアキれてるじゃない！
English: Really, Phoenix! You even got Ms. von Karma to give up on you too!

Japanese: 狩魔検事“も”？
あと、私も。
English: As in, "me too".

Japanese: わ、わかりました。 ‥‥考えなおします。
English: O-Oh... Okay, I'll think more carefully this time...

Japanese: ‥‥ぐッ、このヒトは‥‥！
English: ...Grr... This person...!

Japanese: 倉院の里の人間でなければ、 霊媒師の装束を用意できない。
English: If it wasn't someone from Kurain Village, you couldn't have gotten the costume.

Japanese: 綾里屋敷の人間でなければ、 衣装ばこを用意できない‥‥
English: And if it wasn't someone from the Fey household, you wouldn't have that box to use.

Japanese: アヤサト‥‥キミ子！
English: Morgan... Fey!

Japanese: 先ほどの証人ではないですか！
English: Isn't that the wonderful lady witness we had earlier!?

Japanese: さあ、のどかさん！ 何か言うことはありますか！
English: What do you have to say to this, Ms. Miney!?

Japanese: ‥‥う‥‥ううう‥‥
English: ...Grrr... Grrrrrrrrrrr...

Japanese: あなたは、霧崎医師を撃った！ ‥‥その手で！
English: You shot Dr. Grey with your own two hands!

Japanese: ちがいますかッ！
English: Do you deny it!?

Japanese: きゃああああああああああああああ ああああああああああああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥犯行は、おそらく こんな感じで進められました。
English: I think this is what really happened.

Japanese: 犯人は、あらかじめ 現場にひそんでおきます。
English: The murderer had planted herself at the scene of the crime long beforehand.

Japanese: 霊媒師の装束とカツラをつけて、 被告人に変装して‥‥。
English: Dressed in a medium's costume and wearing a wig, she pretended to be the defendant.

Japanese: やがて、霊媒が始まります。
English: And then, the channeling started.

Japanese: 目を閉じているふたりに、 犯人は足音を殺してしのびよる。
English: The murderer crept silently towards the other two, both of whom had their eyes closed.

Japanese: ‥‥まず、綾里　真宵を 強い睡眠薬で眠らせます。
English: ...First, she drugged Maya Fey with a strong sleeping agent.

Japanese: そして‥‥霧崎先生を、 ナイフで刺した！
English: Then, she stabbed Dr. Grey with the knife!

Japanese: 次に、衣装ばこの中に 被告人をかくす‥‥。
English: Next, she hid my client inside the clothing box...

Japanese: 彼女と入れかわって、 罪を着せるためです。
English: She did that so she could take Maya's place and frame her for the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。そこで手ちがいが 起こった‥‥
English: ...But that's when something unexpected happened.

Japanese: 手ちがい‥‥？
English: Unexpected...?

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Yes.

Japanese: バァァァン！
English: *bang!*

Japanese: 霧崎先生は、 まだ死んでいなかった！
English: Dr. Grey was actually not yet dead!

Japanese: ‥‥最後の力をふりしぼって、 犯人に向かってピストルを撃った！
English: With the last of his strength, he fired a shot at his attacker!

Japanese: だから、ビョウブの弾痕は 低い位置にあったのです！
English: And that is why the hole in the folding screen was so low to the ground!

Japanese: 犯人は霧崎先生から ピストルを奪いとって‥‥
English: The murderer then took the gun from Dr. Grey, and...

Japanese: バァァァン！
English: *bang!*

Japanese: その直後、あなたたちが部屋に 踏みこんだわけですか‥‥
English: After that, you and the other lady thought to break into the room...?

Japanese: おそらく銃声は、計算外の ことだったはずです。
English: Yes, a gunshot is certainly something you wouldn't expect to hear.

Japanese: それを聞いて、ぼくたちは “対面の間”に踏みこみました。
English: Which is why, upon hearing the shots, we forced our way into the Channeling Chamber.
*klik!*

Japanese: ‥‥のどかさんは、とっさに 自分の装束に血をこすりつけて‥‥
English: Ms. Miney... She immediately covered her own costume with blood...
*klik!*

Japanese: 綾里　真宵のフリをしたんです。
English: And pretended to be Maya Fey.

Japanese: で、でも！　‥‥そんなの、 すぐにバレるに決まってる！
English: B-But! That sort of deception would have been easy to see through!

Japanese: ‥‥だから、キミ子さんが せかしたんですよ。
English: Which is precisely why Ms. Morgan Fey chased us out.

Japanese: この場はアタクシに おまかせなさい。
English: Please leave this area to me.

Japanese: ああたたちは、警察に電話を！
English: Go quickly and inform the police!

Japanese: 早くなさいッ！ ‥‥ギセイ者がふえる前に。
English: Hurry! Before there are more victims here!

Japanese: な‥‥ なんということでしょう‥‥
English: Wh-What is one supposed to say...?

Japanese: これが、この事件の真相です。
English: This is the real truth behind this murder.

Japanese: ‥‥フッフッフッフッ‥‥
English: ...Ahahahaha...

Japanese: だ、だれですか！ こんなときに、笑って‥‥
English: Wh-Who is that!? Laughing at a time like this...!?

Japanese: あひッ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: ‥‥ケッサクな連中ね。 ホント、単純な生き物だわ。
English: Oh, you simpleminded fools. I'm sorry, are you still by chance, evolving?

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことだ‥‥？
English: Wh-What is it this time...?

Japanese: か‥‥狩魔検事‥‥ ま、まさか‥‥
English: M-Ms. von Karma... Surely...

Japanese: もちろん！
English: Of course it was me!

Japanese: 狩魔家の人間が、これぐらいの コトで負けを認めると思って？
English: Do you really think someone of Von Karma blood would lose due to something this petty?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだとおぉぉォッ！
English: WHAT IN THE WORLD!?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一！
English: Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: あなたの立証には、 重要な部分が欠けている！
English: Your argument is flawed in one very crucial area!

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: And that is...?

Japanese: この証人が犯人だとしたら‥‥、
English: If this witness is the real murderer...

Japanese: なぜ、そんなメンドウな コトをする必要があったの？
English: why would she go through all this trouble?
...Huh?

Japanese: わざわざ霊媒師と手を組んで、 霊媒師に変装して、シバイして‥‥
English: Working together with a medium, pretending to be one, putting on this whole act...

Japanese: そんなバカげたことをした 理由はなんなの？
English: What reason would she have to do such foolish things?

Japanese: え？　そ、それは‥‥
English: Eh? Well, that's... Uh...

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうよ！ あたしが言いたかったのも、それ！
English: Yeah, that's right! That's exactly what I was gonna say!

Japanese: ‥‥それに、もう１つ。
English: Oh, and one other thing.

Japanese: そもそも、葉中　のどかはなぜ、 霧崎医師を殺害したの？
English: Why would Ms. Ini Miney want to kill Dr. Grey? Where is her motive?

Japanese: そうよ！　あたしも それを言いたかったのよ！
English: Yeah! That's exactly what I wanted to say too!

Japanese: ドーキよ、ドーキ！ あたしにはないわ、そんなの！
English: Yeah! Motive! I don't have a motive!

Japanese: ど‥‥動機。それは‥‥ （ここで“ない”とは言えない！）
English: A... motive... That's... (I can't say she has no motive here!)

Japanese: （今の時点で考えられる動機！ 霧崎先生を殺害した理由は‥‥）
English: (I have to think of a motive now! What is her reason for wanting Dr. Grey dead...?)

Japanese: あなたが霧崎先生を殺害した 理由は‥‥ッ！
English: The reason you wanted Dr. Grey dead is this!

Japanese: やれやれ‥‥。
English: Ho-hum, ho-hum...

Japanese: それのどこがドーキなのよ！
English: Is this a word search? I'm having a tough time finding the word "motive!"

Japanese: うう‥‥なんだか息ギレが‥‥
English: Uuugh... My breathing is becoming labored...

Japanese: 私も、目まいがしてきたわ。
English: My eyesight is failing me...

Japanese: トシはとりたくないものね、 成歩堂　龍一！
English: Makes you not want to get old, doesn't it, Mr. Phoenix Wright?

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ！ 考えなおすか‥‥）
English: (...Ack! I've got to take my time and think carefully about this...)

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥よく思い出すの。
English: Phoenix, think back!

Japanese: のどかさんが、霧崎先生を ウラむとしたら、その理由は‥‥？
English: What kind of ax would Ini have to grind with Dr. Grey?

Japanese: ‥‥１つしか、ないでしょう？
English: There is only one reason, right?
(...Only one...?)

Japanese: ‥‥ハッ！
English: ...Hah!

Japanese: ‥‥予想どおり。 つまんない答えね、成歩堂　龍一。
English: Just as I thought. You bore me with your silly answers, Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: （こいつまでぼくのことを フルネームで呼び始めたぞ‥‥）
English: (Just great. Now even SHE is calling me by my full name...)

Japanese: お姉さんの復讐‥‥ってワケ？
English: You think I did this to get revenge for my sister's death?

Japanese: そ、そうです！
English: Yes!

Japanese: それに、あなた自身も、 あの事故で大ケガをしています！
English: Because through that accident, you suffered a lot of hurt and pain yourself!

Japanese: ‥‥バカバカしい！
English: Don't be stupid!

Japanese: 霧崎が、お姉さんに睡眠薬を 盛った証拠なんて、ないのよ？
English: No one has proof that Dr. Grey drugged my sister, right?

Japanese: それに、復讐したけりゃ さっさとやるわよ。
English: And you want to say I wanted to take revenge based on nothing?

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか‥‥？
English: Wh-What do you mean by that...?

Japanese: ものわかりがワルいジイさんね。
English: Senile, stupid gramps.

Japanese: いいですかあ？
English: I'm, like, going to explain, so, like, please listen, OK?

Japanese: 私が退院したのは、 もう半年も前ですよお。
English: It's been over half a year since I was discharged from the hospital.

Japanese: 復讐に燃えていたんなら、 半年もぼやぼやしてませえん。
English: If I, like, wanted revenge, then, like, I wouldn't have waited this long.

Japanese: ‥‥それに、霊媒のことを 思いついたのは霧崎自身ですよお。
English: Um, and 'sides, like, the guy that thought of the spirit thing, was, like, Dr. Grey.

Japanese: 私が相談されたのは、 グーゼンなんですからねえ。
English: It was, like, a total coincidence he asked me about it, you know?

Japanese: （た、立ちなおられたか‥‥ッ！）
English: (She... She's back to her airhead self...!)

Japanese: し、しかしですね‥‥
English: H-However, you see--

Japanese: あいた！
English: Eeeowch!

Japanese: みっともないわよ ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: Pathetic, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: あなたは、 立証にシッパイしたの。
English: You failed to support your own theory.

Japanese: ‥‥以上。
English: ...The end.

Japanese: どうやら、答えが出たようですな。 ‥‥成歩堂　龍一。
English: I think we have our answer, Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: たしかに、この証人の言うことは もっともです。
English: What Ms. Miney has stated is very true.

Japanese: 復讐にしては計画がフクザツだし、 そもそも動機が見あたらない。
English: The revenge plan is overly complicated, and she has no motive in the first place.

Japanese: 綾里　キミ子が、その犯行に 手を貸す理由すらありません。
